commits.message:
mit-cut-plan-lsh.1: fail loud when the motion grammar is misconfigured

Two silent-failure holes in the new grammar, closed at their single enforcers:

- cssVar() returned '' for a missing CSS token, which would flow into WAAPI as
  an invalid easing string — the animation dies with no error. Now it throws a
  self-describing error the moment the JS↔CSS mirror is broken (a renamed or
  dropped token), covering both WAAPI call sites from one place.
- A misspelled read-beat (e.g. beat:'dwelll') silently fell back to 'read', so
  a scene's timing would lie with no signal. buildNode now validates the beat
  the same way it already validates the painter kind — undefined stays the
  authorised default, a typo fails loudly at construction.

[LAW:no-silent-failure] [LAW:single-enforcer]

mit-cut-plan-lsh.1: motion grammar — one named timing authority

Mint the page's motion vocabulary once and route every scene through it,
replacing the scattered easing/duration literals the facets each invented.

- Easing families + duration scale as CSS :root tokens (--ease-land/-fade,
  --t-tap/-ctl/-veil/-shift, --dur-bob/-blink). Every inline cubic-bezier and
  transition/animation duration now references a token; the only remaining
  literal is the reduced-motion .001ms kill switch (a deliberate exception).
- One source of truth across the CSS/JS divide: the JS MOTION object READS the
  two curve strings WAAPI needs from the CSS tokens (getComputedStyle), so a
  curve is one fact with no twin to drift. The scroll-scrub easing (drift) is
  JS-only and authored once as the exact cubic.  [LAW:one-source-of-truth]
- Read-beat grammar: the compositional invariant bmf asked for — wording holds
  still for an opening beat, then animates; ambient motion may still breathe.
  The INVARIANT is fixed; the LENGTH is a per-scene value from a sanctioned
  palette (brisk/read/dwell). A node names its beat as data; the one paint loop
  turns it into n.w (wording progress) that every painter draws its text from,
  while raw p drives the ambient channel.
  [LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow] [LAW:one-type-per-behavior]

Verified in-browser (chrome-devtools): full-page scroll sweep with zero runtime
errors; grammar mirror resolves (drift 0/.875/1); beats vary across scenes
(dwell 6 / read 19 / brisk 6); the opener's wording holds through its dwell beat
(label "1" until p>.26) then advances, with the final state unchanged.

ci: automatic GitHub Pages deploy via gh-pages-multiplexer (#9)

Assemble the design pages (every */index.html) into a clean gallery dir
with a generated landing page, then deploy to versioned subdirectories on
gh-pages via brandon-fryslie/gh-pages-multiplexer@v1.

- master -> /master/, tags vX -> /vX/, PRs -> /pr-N/ (with sticky preview comment)
- base-path-mode: none — pages are self-contained, links are relative + co-located,
  so no HTML rewriting is needed
- also ignore .DS_Store

mit-verify-cv5.2: contact sheet — gitignore the capture artifacts (#8)

The contact-sheet appearance instrument for the mit-verify-cv5 epic: 31 nodes
× 3 progresses (entry .15 / mid .50 / final .95) = 93 viewport captures into a
single local folder, named by node title + kind + progress, plus a derived
browsable HTML grid (build-index.sh). The rerunnable chrome-devtools MCP
procedure is documented in AGENTS.md.

Per the styles-only repo convention, the only committed output is this
.gitignore rule keeping contact-sheet/ artifacts local — mirroring PR #7's
styles-only enforcement. [LAW:single-enforcer]

Last run: 93/93 scenes captured, 93/93 distinct hashes, full review of all 31
rows, 0 already-broken scenes (no follow-up scene ticket needed).

mit-verify-cv5.1: scroll-through smoke proof + styles-only .gitignore enforcement (#7)

* Verify scroll-through proof; enforce styles-only repo via .gitignore

mit-verify-cv5.1: built and ran the scroll-through smoke proof for
scrollytelling-inception/index.html using the chrome-devtools MCP. The
procedure is documented in AGENTS.md (untracked, per repo convention).

Latest run PASS: 31/31 painter kinds exercised, 31/31 nodes reach p=1
(last node converge=1), 0 measure() drift after fonts.ready, 0 console
messages across 272 instant-scroll steps top->epilogue.

The committed artifact is the .gitignore enforcement: AGENTS.md, .claude/,
.mcp.json, and mcpi.toml are machine-local agent/tooling context, not the
public-domain styles the repo commits. [LAW:single-enforcer] keeps the
convention in one place instead of every agent's memory.

* Install Z.ai coding agent review action

Adds the pull_request-triggered z.ai reviewer so PRs in this repo get an
automated review pass. The ZAI_API_KEY repo secret is provisioned out of
band (macOS keychain -> gh secret set); it is not in the repo.

For same-repo PRs the workflow runs from the head branch, so this commit
also enables the reviewer to run against the PR that introduces it.
Add scroll-pinned-stepper page for Pages deployment (#6)

Retire the last duplicates: new reticle + scatter + deck motifs (strict zero reached) (#5)

* Retire the last duplicates: new reticle + scatter + deck motifs

The final three reused motifs, each kept on its canonical node and replaced on
its in-the-wild/secondary echo:

- annotate stays on 'annotated still'; Reuters becomes reticle — a forensic
  viewfinder that glides and zooms subject to subject, the Reuters/Bellingcat
  visual-tracking form.
- count stays on 'counting up'; The Pudding becomes scatter — points fly up
  into a plotted distribution, swept left to right, their data-essay signature.
- steps-graphic stays on the OPENING (the page's first, purest demo); the
  gallery 'sticky steps' node becomes deck — a 3D card stack that lifts each
  step off the top to reveal the next, a mechanically distinct take on 'a stack
  of steps' than the opener's moving box.

With this, every painter kind is used exactly once: 31 nodes, 31 distinct
motifs, one-to-one. The zero-duplicate-motifs goal is met. [LAW:one-type-per-behavior]

Carries the prior rounds' lessons: deterministic index-based geometry, copy via
textContent, no will-change on the many-element motifs. Verified headless:
0 console errors, 31 nodes, DOM census reports NO duplicates.

* deck: keep the last card as the terminal state

paint scaled f by N, so at the end of the run (p->1) the last card reached
d=-1 and fell into the flung-away branch — the hold spacer showed an empty
stack instead of the final step resting on top. Scale by (N-1) so card 0 is
front at p=0 and the last card is front at p=1, each card landing exactly
once. This matches the first-at-start/last-at-end mapping the other step
painters already use. Verified: at p~0.99 the last card is front, opacity 1.

Retire takeover duplicates: new depth + snow + converge motifs (#4)

takeover was the heaviest reuse (4x): the nesting-demo punchline, the gallery
climax, the NYT citation, and the closing thesis — four different intents
collapsed onto one 'big word, full bleed' painter. That is one-type-per-behavior
in reverse: distinct meanings forced through a shared shape. Strict zero is the
nudge to give each its own true form. [LAW:one-type-per-behavior]

Keep takeover on the gallery 'full-bleed' node, where 'it takes the whole
screen' is the technique demonstrating itself. Give the other three their own:

- depth: an endless 3D dive through nested, recycling window-frames — the
  nesting punchline made literal ('as deep as the page is tall'). Inside the
  framed chrome, so the tunnel IS the page's own nesting.
- snow: parallax snowfall behind a serif masthead — the NYT 'Snow Fall' homage,
  in motion ('Snow Fall · The New York Times · 2012').
- converge: SVG threads weave from the edges into one point as the closing line
  resolves at the center ('Scroll in. Picture out.').

Each is one data-driven registry entry; the recursive engine is untouched.
[LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow] Carrying the prior rounds' lessons: deterministic
index-based geometry (no Math.random), copy via textContent, and no will-change
on the many-element motifs (12 rings, 46 flakes transform per frame; the writes
composite on demand — an always-on hint at that count isn't justified).

takeover: 4x -> 1x. Verified headless: 0 console errors, all three render.

Retire scaleword duplicates: new cascade + focus motifs (#3)

* Retire scaleword duplicates: new cascade + focus motifs

scaleword was the first reuse in scroll order (3x: the nesting-demo opener,
the gallery header, the in-the-wild header). Keep it on the framed opener
where the scale-punch belongs; give the two section headers their own motifs:

- cascade: characters rise and fade into place left-to-right, paced by scroll
  (kinetic typography) — 'the forms / wearing many faces'.
- focus: the phrase pulls from blur into sharp focus, a focus rack on the
  words — 'in the wild / everyone bends it'.

Same two-line data shape, distinct mechanics -> two painters, not a style
flag on scaleword. [LAW:one-type-per-behavior] The engine is untouched; only
the registry and two nodes' kind changed. [LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow]

cascade chars are namespaced .cs-c, not .ch — .ch is the chart container's
class, and the unscoped name leaked chart's absolute/flex rules onto every
glyph. Class names are a shared namespace; the collision is a source-of-truth
break avoided by prefixing. [LAW:one-source-of-truth]

scaleword: 3x -> 1x. Verified headless: 0 console errors, both motifs render.

* cascade: drop per-glyph will-change

will-change on every character span permanently promotes ~30 small elements
to compositor layers for no benefit — the per-frame transform/opacity writes
already composite them when they actually move. A performance knob with no
value case at this granularity; removed. Single-element uses (focus, zoom,
takeover) keep theirs, where the hint is justified.

* cascade + focus: set copy as text, not markup

Both painters interpolated node.steps into innerHTML, so a literal < or & in
authored copy would parse as markup/entities — the data is display text, but
the channel was HTML. That mismatch is an invariant ('no < or & in a step')
encoded in no type. Build the span structure, then assign each glyph/line via
textContent so copy renders verbatim and the illegal state is unrepresentable.
[LAW:types-are-the-program]

This also drops the   entity hack: white-space:pre on .cs-c already
preserves spaces, so a plain textContent=ch suffices. Render unchanged;
0 console errors.

* cascade/focus: name ctx fields by role, not by collision

ctx.n (cascade char count) and ctx.p (focus element) read against the
paint(p,n) params n and p — different scopes, but the eye trips on n.ctx.n
and n.ctx.p. Rename to ctx.count and ctx.el so each field announces its role.
No behavior change.

Retire terminal duplicate: new trigger motif for the observe node (#2)

* Retire terminal duplicate: new trigger motif for the observe node

The observe node and the sticky node both named the terminal painter — the
first motif reuse you hit scrolling. Retire it by giving observe its own
motif: a trip line at the viewport center with step chips rising through it,
each firing (lighting up) as it crosses. The IntersectionObserver form made
literal — the dashed line IS the rootMargin trigger.

One painter added to the registry, one node's kind flipped; the recursive
engine is untouched. Variety is data, so de-duplicating it is a data edit.

terminal: 2x -> 1x. Verified headless: 0 console errors.

* trigger motif: single-source the sensor-line position

The line's vertical position was encoded twice — CSS .tg-line top and the
painter's chip math — two copies of one concept that could drift, lighting
chips while they cross empty space. Author it once in build (line/span
locals): JS->CSS via the --tg-line custom property for the dashed line,
JS->math via ctx for the chips. Flow is one-way so no per-frame
getComputedStyle read sneaks back in. [LAW:one-source-of-truth]

Render unchanged; 0 console errors.

Scrollytelling field guide: self-demonstrating nested scrollytelling (#1)

* Add scrollytelling field guide: self-demonstrating nested scrollytelling

A long-form explainer about scrollytelling that demonstrates the concept
through genuinely nested sticky scrollytellers. One recursive engine paints
a tree of nodes; depth is the tree's shape, variety is which painter each
node names, and native CSS sticky stacking does the nesting (ancestors pin
while descendants pin on top, LIFO unwind on exit).

- Canonical full-bleed opening with no hint it continues
- A bounded ~4-screen 'nested windows' demo, then full-bleed for good
- ~20 distinct painter motifs (steps, runway, sequence, horizontal, chart,
  parallax, mask, wipe, draw, count, typewriter, takeover, ring, scaleword,
  terminal, shapemorph, zoom, waffle, annotate, explode)
- Chrome treatment is explicit node data (framed vs bleed), reset on bleed
  so window chrome can't leak past the demo

* Retire ring and terminal duplicate motifs

Two more motifs toward zero-duplicate variety:
- gauge: a semicircular dial with a sweeping needle, replacing the ring
  reuse at the runway/pin-length node (reads as a duration meter)
- bind: a scroll->animation wiring diagram with an energizing connector and
  traveling pulse, replacing the CSS-scroll-timeline terminal (shows the
  native binding better than code, and breaks the back-to-back terminal pair)

Each removal is one registry entry plus one node 'kind' flip; the engine,
tick loop, and tree builder are untouched.

* Respect prefers-reduced-motion; cache canvas backing store; fix #top anchor

Accessibility: the OS reduced-motion preference is now honoured from one
source. A CSS @media query neutralises all CSS animation/transition and
sets scroll-behavior:auto; a single JS REDUCE_MOTION constant (reading the
same setting) gates the two WAAPI entrance flourishes. Text content always
updates — only the decorative animation varies by preference.

Performance: frame-sequence cached its 2D context and backing-store size,
reallocating the canvas only when client size x devicePixelRatio changes
instead of every scroll tick.

Correctness: the hero now carries id="top" so the epilogue's back-to-top
anchor has a valid target. The anchor is now a plain href="#top" — the
smooth glide comes from CSS scroll-behavior (auto under reduced motion),
so the inline onclick handler is gone.

* Eliminate per-frame layout thrashing; assert painter kinds resolve

Performance: tick() read getBoundingClientRect() + offsetHeight for all 31
nodes interleaved with style writes every scroll frame — each read forced a
synchronous re-layout of what the prior write just dirtied. Node geometry
(document-space top + run height) is now a derived cache, recomputed only on
resize/load; tick() reads scrollY once and computes progress arithmetically,
so the read and write phases never interleave. No viewport-skip branch was
added — the thrashing was the bottleneck, not the arithmetic, and a skip
conditional would put control-flow back into a clean dataflow loop.

Robustness: buildNode now throws a self-describing error naming the node and
unknown kind if node.kind misses the painter registry, instead of crashing
cryptically at painter.build(). Fails loud and localised, not silent.

* Add noscript fallback; mark decorative canvas aria-hidden

Accessibility/robustness:
- The experience is entirely JS-rendered into #doc, so a <noscript> now states
  plainly that JavaScript is required and what the page does. Explicit failure,
  not a silent blank page.
- The frame-sequence canvas is decorative illustration; its meaning is already
  carried by the scene caption. Marked aria-hidden="true" so screen readers
  skip it rather than announcing an unlabelled canvas or a fabricated label that
  would duplicate the caption's text.

* Track live reduced-motion; nullish ownVh default; drop roundRect; remeasure on fonts.ready

- REDUCE_MOTION now tracks the live media query via a change listener, so
  toggling the OS preference mid-read keeps JS flourishes in sync with CSS
  (the live query is the source of truth, not a load-time snapshot).
- node.ownVh default uses ?? so an intentional 0 is honoured; only an absent
  value falls back to 1.4.
- frame-sequence draws plain fillRect slabs instead of ctx.roundRect(), which
  Safari 16.0-16.3 lacks despite supporting the container queries this page
  needs — deleting the dependency beats shimming it; the corner radius was
  decorative.
- geometry cache is now also invalidated on document.fonts.ready, since web
  fonts can reflow layout after the load event and desync cached node tops.
